name: Spicy Thai Basil Chicken with Egg
instructions:
1. Lightly coat a large skillet with cooking spray and place over medium heat. Crack the eggs into the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until scrambled and fully set, about 5 minutes. Divide the cooked rice evenly between two serving bowls and top each with the scrambled eggs.

2. Using a mortar and pestle or food processor, grind the Thai chile peppers and garlic cloves together into a coarse paste, releasing their aromatic oils.

3. Wipe out the skillet, coat it again with cooking spray, and heat over medium-high heat. Add the chile-garlic paste and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant and the garlic turns golden brown, about 1 minute. Be careful not to burn it.

4. Add the diced chicken breast to the skillet and cook, stirring frequently, until the chicken is about halfway cooked through and starting to turn opaque, about 3 minutes.

5. Stir in the soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar, mixing well to coat the chicken. Continue cooking and stirring until the chicken is fully cooked and no longer pink in the center, about 3 more minutes.

6. Add the fresh basil leaves and stir for just 10 seconds. Remove the skillet from heat and continue stirring until the basil wilts from the residual heat. Spoon the chicken mixture generously over the rice and eggs, and serve immediately.
simpleInstructions:
1. Scramble eggs in a sprayed skillet, then divide rice into bowls and top with eggs.

2. Grind chiles and garlic into a paste, then sauté until golden and aromatic.

3. Stir-fry chicken until halfway done, then add soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sugar.

4. Toss in fresh basil for 10 seconds, let it wilt, then spoon over rice and dig in!
